qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: PathPrefix route confusion allows /../ to bypass protected frontend middleware
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a raw-path PathPrefix routing confusion caused by SkipClean(true) and prefix matching on r.URL.Path; the advisory is a ForwardAuth subrequest header-handling flaw where attacker-controlled X-Forwarded-Prefix survives despite trustForwardHeader=false.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory root cause. The accepted session finding bypasses BasicAuth through raw-path PathPrefix route selection on unclean paths, while the advisory requires ForwardAuth behind a trusted upstream proxy and attacker-controlled X-Forwarded-Prefix reaching the auth service despite trustForwardHeader=false.
evidence: The advisory describes pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go copying request headers into the auth subrequest and failing to strip or rebuild X-Forwarded-Prefix when trustForwardHeader=false.

The advisory's exploit narrative depends on ForwardAuth, a trusted upstream proxy, and an auth service making authorization decisions from X-Forwarded-Prefix, often after StripPrefix adds a second prefix value.
The finding report instead identifies repo/server/server_configuration.go:639 (SkipClean(true)) and repo/rules/rules.go:96 (raw strings.HasPrefix on r.URL.Path) as the vulnerable mechanism.

The finding reproduction configures Traefik v1.7.34 with two PathPrefix frontends and BasicAuth on /admin; it does not configure ForwardAuth, trustForwardHeader=false, a trusted upstream proxy, or any X-Forwarded-Prefix-based authorization flow.
